title: My name is missing from the voter list
subtitle: Diagnose what happened and fix it

intro.title: This is more common than you think.
intro.body: Names go missing for many reasons — and most are fixable. Let's figure out what happened to yours and what to do.

verify.title: Try every search method first.
verify.body: Sometimes the name is there but searched wrong — wrong spelling, wrong constituency, or your EPIC works but not your name.

choices.label: Definitely not there
choices.sublabel: I tried EPIC, name, and address

foundIt.title: Great — you're registered.
foundIt.summary: Save your booth details and EPIC number. We recommend setting an election day reminder so you don't forget on the day.

diagnose.eyebrow: Step 3 — Why might it be missing?
diagnose.title: Pick what most likely applies to you.

choices.label: I never registered
choices.sublabel: Or I'm not sure if I did

choices.label: I was registered before but it's gone

neverRegistered.title: You'll need to file Form 6.
neverRegistered.body: Form 6 is for new voters. It's the same form first-time voters use. Takes about 8 minutes to complete online.

moved.title: You need to register in your new constituency.
moved.body:
If you moved to a different assembly constituency, you must file Form 6 at your new address. Your old registration will be deleted automatically once the new one is approved.

If you only moved within the same constituency, file Form 8 instead (correction).

deleted.title: Your name was likely removed during a roll revision.
deleted.body:
This happens during Special Summary Revision drives if a BLO couldn't verify your address. The fix is to file Form 6 again with current address proof.

Also talk to your **BLO directly** — they can investigate why you were marked for deletion.

form6Action.title: File Form 6 now.
form6Action.body:
Make sure you have:
- Aadhaar or other age proof
- Recent address proof
- Passport-size photograph

afterAction.title: Submitted. Now follow up.
afterAction.summary: Track your application status weekly. Contact your BLO via the Voter Helpline app if you don't see progress in 2 weeks. Election deadlines are strict — typically 30 days before polling day.
